residencyPermitContent.title: Student Residence Permit
residencyPermitContent.description: A mandatory residence permit type for foreign students pursuing associate, undergraduate, graduate, or doctoral education in Turkey.
residencyPermitContent.importantInfoText1: Student residence permit applications are made through the
residencyPermitContent.importantInfoText2: system. After filling out the application form, you may need to submit it along with the required documents to your university's International Student Office (or the unit specified by the Immigration Office) within
residencyPermitContent.warningText2: for open education or distance learning programs. You must physically attend education in Turkey.

requiredDocuments.title: Application Form
requiredDocuments.desc: Wet-signed form obtained from the e-Residence system.

requiredDocuments.title: Passport
requiredDocuments.desc: Original and photocopies of processed pages.

requiredDocuments.title: Student Certificate
requiredDocuments.desc: Current wet or e-signed document showing active student status.

requiredDocuments.title: Biometric Photo
requiredDocuments.desc: 4 photos taken within the last 6 months with a white background.

requiredDocuments.title: Health Insurance
requiredDocuments.desc: A valid private health insurance policy in Turkey.

requiredDocuments.title: Address Document
requiredDocuments.desc: Notarized rental contract or dormitory document.

requiredDocuments.title: Card Fee Receipt
requiredDocuments.desc: Tax office receipt proving payment of the valuable paper fee.

requiredDocuments.title: UETS Document
requiredDocuments.desc: Notification address document obtained via e-Government or PTT.

residencyPermitContent.processChangeDepartmentDesc: Notification must be made within 20 days for faculty/department changes within the same city. If transferring to a different city, a new application must be submitted to that city's Immigration Office within 10 days.
residencyPermitContent.processFreezeRegistrationDesc: Residence permits of students who freeze their registration for any reason are canceled. When returning to education, a new initial application must be made.
residencyPermitContent.faq1Question: What happens to my residence permit after graduation?
residencyPermitContent.faq1Answer: Your student residence permit ends on the graduation date. If you plan to stay in Turkey, you must switch to a 'Short-Term Residence Permit' within 10 days (application can be made within 6 months after graduation).
residencyPermitContent.faq2Question: Can I study and work at the same time?
residencyPermitContent.faq2Answer: A student residence permit does not directly grant work rights. Associate and undergraduate students can apply for part-time work permits after their first year. Graduate and doctoral students can obtain work permits without time restrictions.
residencyPermitContent.faq3Question: Do I need to pay a fee?
residencyPermitContent.faq3Answer: Foreigners are exempt from the 'Residence Permit Fee' when applying for a student residence permit. Only the annually determined 'Residence Permit Card Fee' must be paid.
residencyPermitContent.faq4Question: What should I do if my address changes?
residencyPermitContent.faq4Answer: You are legally required to notify the Population Directorate first and then the Provincial Immigration Office of your updated address within 20 business days after any change in your address or contact information. Failure to do so may result in a penalty.
residencyPermitContent.ctaDescription: Our expert team is with you for university registration, equivalency, and residence permit applications. Minimize your risks.

workPermitContent.title: Student Work Permit
workPermitContent.description: Work rights, application processes, and critical legal details for foreign students studying in Turkey.
workPermitContent.legalRightsParagraph1: The conditions for foreign students' work permits are regulated by {law}. Work rights and limits are defined according to the level of education.
workPermitContent.associateBachelorDesc: Applications can be made after the {firstYear} and {partTime} work rights are granted.
workPermitContent.masterDoctorateDesc: Applications can be made from the beginning of the education and {noLimit} does not apply.
workPermitContent.importantNoteDesc: A student residence permit alone does not grant work rights. Every student wishing to work must apply for a Work Permit through an official employer.
workPermitContent.minWageEmploymentDesc: Employers may not need to meet foreign employment quotas (e.g., 5 Turkish citizens per foreign worker) in some exceptional cases, but professional application reduces the risk of rejection.
workPermitContent.studentEndDesc: If your student status ends for any reason (graduation, registration freeze, deregistration, etc.), your student residence permit will be canceled, affecting the legal status of your work permit.
workPermitContent.howToApplyStep2: An agreement must be made with an employer, and the company official who is an E-Declaration user must upload the application to the Ministry of Labor and Social Security system.
workPermitContent.howToApplyStep3: Avoid rejection in this high-risk process by completing your application flawlessly with the support of Atasa Education experts.
workPermitContent.faqQuestion1: Why can't I apply in the first year?
workPermitContent.faqAnswer1: By law, associate and bachelor students are not granted legal work rights in their first year to focus on adaptation and education. However, there is no such restriction for master's and doctoral students.
workPermitContent.faqQuestion2: Will my residence permit change if I get a work permit?
workPermitContent.faqAnswer2: No, a valid work permit also serves as a residence permit. However, maintaining your rights related to school processes is important. Attention should be paid when obtaining a work permit outside of education.
workPermitContent.faqQuestion3: Can my employer cancel my permit without my knowledge?
workPermitContent.faqAnswer3: Your employer is obliged to notify the Ministry within 15 days if you leave the job. After notification, your permit period is canceled. If you want to work again, a new application must be made by your new employer.
workPermitContent.ctaDesc: Contact Atasa Consultants to avoid rejection in official procedures between your employer and the Ministry.

applySteps.title: Become a University Student in 8 Easy Steps!
applySteps.description: With Atasa Education, university applications are now very easy. Our consultants will be with you every step of the way.
applySteps.step01Desc: To apply, you must first complete the Application Form fully. Upload the required documents through the relevant form. If you haven't decided which university to apply to yet, selecting the departments you are interested in is sufficient.
applySteps.step02Desc: Once your application reaches us, our consultants will contact you via WhatsApp, email, or phone to inform you about your application and the process.
applySteps.step03Desc: For more detailed information, you can visit our office and benefit from the free consultancy services provided by our education experts. Our consultants will assist you with university and department selection, quota status, payment terms, and many other matters.
applySteps.step04Desc: Based on your preferences and criteria, acceptance letters from our partner universities will be sent to you via email. These acceptance letters are not binding, meaning you are not obligated to register. You can consider these letters as offers.
applySteps.step05Desc: If you find the accepted university and department suitable, you need to make the tuition payment to the official bank accounts of the university as stated in the offer letter. You can pay the tuition fee in full for the entire year or as a deposit at the beginning.
applySteps.step06Desc: After making the payment to the university, don't forget to send your payment receipt to your consultant via WhatsApp or email. Your consultant will share the receipt with the university to expedite the process.
applySteps.step07Desc: Once your tuition payment reaches the university's bank account, the university will send your official acceptance letter to your email address.
applySteps.step08Desc: Prepare your official acceptance letter and all necessary documents for registration, visit your university, and complete your registration.

serviceList.description: We manage all official procedures in Turkey under one roof with a professional and reliable approach.
serviceList.residencyDesc: Consultancy and assistance services for obtaining residence permits that allow you to stay in Turkey during your education.
serviceList.workDesc: We guide you through the necessary work permit applications if you want to work in Turkey alongside your education.
serviceList.citizenshipDesc: Consultancy services for individuals considering long-term stay and Turkish citizenship in Turkey.
serviceList.studentDesc: We provide support at every stage of the education process for students coming to Turkey from abroad for education, including university registrations, student visas, and equivalency procedures.
serviceList.consultancyDesc: We offer personalized solutions for every student and foreign citizen, providing one-on-one support with academic consultancy, career planning, cultural adaptation programs, and more.

applyWhyUs.title: Why Should You Apply with Atasa Education?
applyWhyUs.reasonUniversityRepDesc: Atasa Education operates as the official representative of universities. We are not intermediaries; we are directly affiliated with universities.
applyWhyUs.reasonFullAcceptanceDesc: Our success rate is 100% because we are unique in our field. Become a university student with Atasa Education.
applyWhyUs.reasonDiplomaDesc: We offer placement to all partner universities without exams and based on diploma grades.
applyWhyUs.reasonStudentsDesc: Atasa Education has helped over 10,000 foreign students with their university admission processes in Turkey. Now it's your turn!
applyWhyUs.reasonScholarshipsDesc: All foreign students applying to universities through Atasa Education benefit from special discounts.
applyWhyUs.reasonNoFeeDesc: As official representatives of universities, we do not charge any consultancy fees. Applications are completely free.

applyAdvantages.titleHighlight: Privileges of Applying with Atasa Education
applyAdvantages.description: We are here to provide you with the best support on your education journey in Turkey. Here are some of the advantages we offer:
applyAdvantages.advantage1: Applications are free. We are here to guide you. We look forward to helping you achieve your educational goals.
applyAdvantages.advantage2: You can automatically benefit from special discounts exclusive to Atasa Education. Additionally, you have access to special scholarship opportunities offered only through us.
applyAdvantages.advantage3: You can apply to multiple universities simultaneously with a single application form. Your acceptance letters will be sent directly to your email by the universities.
applyAdvantages.advantage4: You can benefit from special quotas, receive visa support if necessary, and also get assistance with dormitory or accommodation.
applyAdvantages.advantage5: Our experienced consultants will help you make the right department and university choices.
applyAdvantages.advantage6: We support you at every stage of the process until you start university.
applyAdvantages.advantage7: Apply with the official university representative!
applyAdvantages.advantage8: We offer exam-free admission to universities based solely on your diploma grade.
